title: Work Package Write Draft Packet History Demotion Execution
objective: Normalize the remaining live-looking 2026-04-14 PM work-package write-surface packet-definition singleton so that file no longer presents itself as a still-live PM work-package write packet after standalone repo cutover and instead acts as historical packet provenance with an explicit current-routing redirect.
dependency_note: After Packet 241 closes the adjacent `pm-schema-012i` singleton, the nearest remaining current-looking residue in the same PM domain draft lane is the successor `pm-schema-013` work-package write-surface singleton. Its surrounding packet-history now serves only as provenance, so the packet-definition JSON itself is the strongest adjacent live-looking residue.
execution_scope: Retitle the `pm-schema-013` packet-definition singleton as historical provenance.
Add explicit `historical_note` and `current_routing` fields to the packet JSON.
Preserve the original packet body below those top-entry routing fields without reopening write-surface implementation, runtime, or schema scope.
forbidden_scope: broad packet-archive rewriting beyond top-entry demotion and routing normalization
success_criteria: The `pm-schema-013` packet-definition singleton now reads as historical provenance instead of a live PM work-package write packet.
The file explicitly routes current readers to `PROJECT_STATUS.md` and the closeout inventory.
Repo-owned status routing records the closure and leaves no ambiguity about the next remaining adjacent residue check.
next_action: Reassess any smaller adjacent packet-history surfaces that still read as current after the `pm-schema-013` singleton closure.
